symptoms.symptom_name: Severe suprapubic bladder pain with frequency/urgency, voiding drops

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suprapubic pain with filling/voiding and marked frequency is the core IC/BPS phenotype.
Dysuria is non-specific; upper-tract features are absent.
Absence of fever/rigors lowers pyelonephritis probability, though it cannot be fully excluded without urine testing.

symptoms.symptom_name: No flank/loin pain or loin-to-groin radiation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Strictly suprapubic pain without loin-to-groin radiation argues strongly against an obstructing calculus.
No costovertebral-angle pain lowers upper-tract infection probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Passing small volumes (not complete retention), no gross clots

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continued voiding of small volumes argues against complete acute retention.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain-driven autonomic arousal (fast heart rate/breathing) fits a severe flare; sepsis would usually add fever.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lower abdominal allodynia and pelvic-floor spasm

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Allodynia and pelvic-floor spasm reflect central sensitization characteristic of IC/BPS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urine dipstick: trace haematuria, negative nitrites/leukocytes (investigation)

loinc_ref.display: Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative nitrites/leukocytes with trace haematuria; MCS expected sterile — supports a non-infective bladder flare but must be confirmed.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot exclude UTI/pyelonephritis without urinalysis and culture; obtain via in-person pathway.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative nitrites/leukocytes reduce UTI probability, though a culture is definitive.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sterile urine with bladder pain supports IC/BPS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic tenderness/hyperesthesia, no CVA tenderness, normal genitalia (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suprapubic tenderness without CVA tenderness fits a lower-tract/bladder process.
Absence of CVA tenderness argues against upper-tract infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Established interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Known IC/BPS is the substrate for a recurrent bladder pain flare.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome flare
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe suprapubic pain with frequency/urgency, allodynia and pelvic-floor spasm, afebrile, no flank pain, still voiding, with negative nitrites/leukocytes, indicates a sterile IC/BPS flare — but infection, obstruction and retention cannot be excluded remotely and require in-person urinalysis/assessment before strong sedatives are given.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude/confirm UTI and guide treatment; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person abdominal/bladder examination and bladder scan if retention suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess for tenderness, retention and CVA tenderness.
Only if a stone or obstruction is suspected on assessment; not a first remote step.
